text:
MssCorp (https://t.co/MMqfwsnwyH)  2026/5/27 earnings presentation leak :

-Self-developed MSS HG (Helmet Gecko) Silicon Photonics tester:

• Three HG units have been assembled and are now operational, providing silicon photonics R&D analysis and engineering validation services to multiple clients.
• The core optical loss detection technology has received patents in Taiwan, Japan, and the United States.
• The system has successfully completed certification by major US AI chip manufacturers.
• PD and QA production versions are scheduled for launch in 2026.
• High-volume commercial ramp-up is expected in 2027.
This development represents one of the company’s four key growth engines, advancing the transition from analysis services to a higher-margin business model encompassing equipment sales, IP licensing, and integrated measurement services in the expanding CPO/silicon photonics market.

Full presentation deck: https://t.co/RVLtv96uSD

@th3Oneorac3d Active alignment is slow - it can take minutes per unit, which kills your throughput. POET's passive approach runs at standard semiconductor speeds, so you're talking seconds instead of minutes. That's huge for volume production.

Roughly 2 orders of magnitude (100x) improvement

POET's passive approach runs at standard semiconductor speeds, so you're talking seconds instead of minutes. That's huge for volume production. Roughly 2 orders of magnitude (100x) improvement per device in effective alignment/assembly time. ￼ Why ~2 orders of magnitude? • Traditional active alignment (most photonics competitors): Requires powering up the device + real-time feedback loops to optimize coupling. This is typically done serially (one module at a time) and takes tens of seconds to several minutes per device/module. ￼ • POET passive alignment: Mechanically self-aligning via pre-etched features on the wafer. Uses standard semiconductor pick-and-place tools — ~0.1–few seconds per component placement, done in a highly parallel wafer-level process (500+ engines per 8” wafer)."}}}, "rest_id": "2058429967827079439", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2728", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2059055899705332000", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "127", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Feels like the market is hunting for anything tied to the AI infrastructure and photonics buildout right now, especially smaller high beta names.

Interesting seeing capital rotate further down the stack too. $XMAX has started popping up more for a similar reason with the AI infrastructure narrative plus the indirect SpaceX and xAI exposure layered on top.

$LPTH deep dive:

LightPath is a real little defense/edge-optics story, not just another “AI photonics” ticker someone found under a couch cushion.

Q3 FY26 revenue was USD 19.1M, +109% YoY. Gross profit was USD 7.0M, +161%. Gross margin hit 36%, adjusted EBITDA was positive at USD 1.1M, and backlog reached roughly USD 110.6M, up 196% from June 2025.

The thesis: BlackDiamond germanium-free infrared optics, G5 imaging systems, and U.S. optical manufacturing could become useful in counter-drone, ISR, border, weapons, and autonomous sensing programs. This is physical AI / defense-sensor plumbing, not data-center CPO.

The data is good enough to care. TTM revenue is only about USD 62.8M, but backlog is already more than 1.7x TTM sales. Management is targeting more than USD 300M of revenue within five years. Consensus has FY26 revenue around USD 72.7M and FY27 around USD 101.3M, so estimate revisions are the actual battleground.

Top catalysts I care about:

1. Q4/FY27 guide proving backlog converts into revenue and margin.
2. A named defense program or prime/customer order above USD 20M.
3. BlackDiamond / second U.S. glass capacity turning from slide-deck promise into qualified throughput and orders.

Risks: the stock already ripped to USD 16.95 after a +18.7% day, above the USD 15.98 average sell-side target and basically at the USD 17 high target. GAAP losses and negative FCF still matter. Defense procurement can turn “pipeline” into “please wait 18 months.” And the social signal is still mostly single-source.

Entry/Verdict: CATALYST WATCH / no chase. Optional 0.25%-0.50% scout only if you want defense-optics optionality. Add-size only after backlog conversion, >35% gross margin, positive adjusted EBITDA, or a named USD 20M+ defense order.

I like the proof. I hate paying victory-lap prices before the next contract shows up. We'll see how it goes.

Canonical Ledger Entry

⚖️ Embedded Photonics in Vehicles — Science vs. Speculation

Let’s separate what is scientifically plausible from what remains unproven.

Embedded photonics in vehicles is not science fiction.

Modern vehicles already contain:
• LiDAR systems
• infrared sensing
• radar arrays
• optical cameras
• laser-based range finding
• fiber-optic communications
• HUD projection systems
• adaptive lighting systems
• driver monitoring cameras
• occupancy sensors
• gesture recognition
• and autonomous navigation architectures

These are all forms of applied photonics:
the science of generating, transmitting, detecting, or manipulating light.

🧠 Why Automakers Use Embedded Photonics:

Legitimate applications include:
• collision avoidance
• autonomous driving
• lane detection
• facial/eye tracking for safety
• interior occupancy mapping
• adaptive headlights
• augmented reality HUD overlays
• V2X communication systems
• and environmental sensing

⚖️ Witness Science Observation:

As vehicles become:
• connected
• sensor-dense
• AI-assisted
• and continuously networked

they increasingly function less like mechanical machines and more like:
mobile sensor platforms.

That shift matters.

Especially when:
• telemetry
• behavioral analytics
• geolocation
• and environmental mapping

become economically valuable.

🧠 Important Scientific Boundary:

The existence of embedded photonics in vehicles does NOT prove:
• targeting systems
• weaponization
• behavioral manipulation
• or covert surveillance operations

However:
the underlying sensing architecture itself is real, documented, and expanding rapidly across the automotive industry.

⚖️ What Investigators Should Scientifically Examine:

If anomalous vehicle behavior is alleged, investigators should review:
• telemetry logs
• sensor synchronization
• LiDAR/radar outputs
• occupancy sensor anomalies
• network traffic
• CAN bus activity
• wireless module communications
• HUD/projector systems
• camera calibration records
• and firmware integrity

Because in modern vehicles:
light itself is part of the operating system.

Evidence. Integrity. Survival.

text: Been watching photonics since late last year, I’ve never initiated any positions. Do I regret it?Kinda, but it’s all discipline investing. I am more of long term holder than a pump now dump after a month or so. Congrats to all those gains, learn to take profits if you need to!😉

This Friday, May 29th, the 5th International Photonics & Quantum Technologies Job Fair 2026 will take place in the Physics Building at the Warsaw University of Technology. We cordially invite you. More information is available at: https://t.co/3Uo9ZMTQwu https://t.co/CbiEvxfP4t
Photonics is an analog substrate. Transformers require an electronic one. Trying to fit the latter in the former is a FANTASY.

text:
AI clusters face hard limits on how many accelerators you can connect before power and latency kill the gains. Lightmatter is bringing 3D photonics and THz optical fabrics out of the lab and into real data center designs.

This changes the game for scaling. Optical I/O slashes energy per bit moved across racks and enables denser, more efficient AI factories. Next-gen models could train faster and cheaper as interconnect bottlenecks ease, putting pressure on traditional electrical networking suppliers while opening doors for photonic specialists.

Long $SIVE $SIVEF

text:
$SIVE - The InP supply chain reality most AI investors miss:

Silicon photonics handles routing & modulation.
It CANNOT produce light.

Every AI optical interconnect, every co-packaged optics (CPO) system, every silicon photonic data center stack needs an EXTERNAL Indium Phosphide (InP) laser as the light source.

→ Sub-30% InP wafer yields industry-wide
→ Only 3 scaled Western suppliers: IQE (UK), Coherent (US), Sivers (Sweden)
→ Demand running ahead of available production
→ Sivers is the most concentrated InP pure-play of the three

This is the picks-and-shovels chokepoint in AI photonics.🔥

text: The big issue for Nvidia is what happens after Feynman+. They have reached the literal limits of physics after 0.5nm. There is nothing there. Just reams and reams of GPUs optimized by photonics, glass, chip design etc. Any kind of optimization on a stagnating slope of raw processing will lose to Custom ASICs, this is common sense really.

text:
Both are AIM-listed deep tech plays riding the same photonics and AI wave.
But they are different animals in my view. 
$SIVE is a chip designer and manufacturer. Revenue already at scale. Moving fast.
$ENSI is an ASIC design house. The revenue ramp is earlier stage, tied to chips converting from design to supply.
Could ENSI follow SIVE's trajectory? The pipeline suggests yes. The timeline is longer.
If you have patience, the setup is arguably more asymmetric.

text:
Huge move, but this is exactly where discipline matters.

The story is strong: AI data centres, photonics, US listing potential, and a growing pipeline.

But after a 3,000% run, we shift towards "how much of the future is already priced in?"

Pipeline ≠ revenue yet. I’d be watching execution, margins, cash burn, and the insider investigation closely.

text:
A team at Westlake University in China just published a handheld cancer detector in Nature Photonics. Single drop of blood, 94.9% accuracy for early-stage lung cancer, 10,000× more sensitive than the standard lab test.

Chip cost: $5.Worth paying attention to, but also worth reading carefully. That 94.9% comes from 171 patient samples. The device replaces refrigerator-sized lab equipment with an LED, a photodetector, and a 3D metamaterial chip. Genuinely impressive physics.

However, the gap nobody's talking about: every year produces 3-4 "revolutionary cancer detection" papers. Almost none survive the jump from Nature paper to clinical validation at scale.

Caveat here: 171 patients is a starting point, not proof. But the engineering approach is what's worth watching.

text:
The AI bottlenecks that are forming as we speak

$MRVL supplies critical connectivity silicon. $COHR is critical for its industrial-scale photonics manufacturing. Big backing by $NVDA. $LITE is critical for its continuous wave laser components and early high-speed lane adoption. Also backed by $NVDA.

For bandwidth and capacity limits, $ALAB has a dominant position for scaling up AI clusters.

For cooling, $VRT has been the dominate leader for the data center cooling market.

For grid exhaustion, $CEG is the leader in dedicated nuclear generation and is the largest pure-play carbon-free nuclear operator. $VST has decade-long agreements with hyperscalers too for nuclear power. $OKLO is also a strong contender for dedicated nuclear generation.

$AVGO is the critical enabler of the ASIC revolution. They are engineering custom silicon to minimize power and interconnect penalties. 

$CBRS is proposing a way to bypass the interconnect wall by utilizing wafer-scale alternatives. This bypasses the need for co-packaged optics by manufacturing AI chips as a single, massive silicon wafers, which keeps the pathways fully on chip.

text:
THE PHOTONICS LAYER OF THE AI BUILDOUT

Goldman Sachs says the AI optical networking market growing nearly 10x to $150B+ by 2028 as clusters scale into multi-rack systems like $NVDA Rubin Ultra NVL576.

The AI bottleneck is shifting to the fabric that lets hundreds of GPUs behave like one system driving a massive dollar content expansion across NVLink, co-packaged optics & optical interconnects: 

• $AEHR burn-in & reliability test systems for photonics and AI hardware
• $AAOI data center optical transceivers with in-house laser manufacturing
• $LITE high-speed lasers & optical components for data center transceivers
• $VIAV optical testing & measurement tools used to validate high-speed networks
• $COHR lasers, photonic components & materials used across optical networking systems
• $MRVL optical DSPs, custom interconnect silicon & networking chips for AI infrastructure
• $CRDO retimers, DSPs & active electrical cables for rack-level signal integrity in AI clusters https://t.co/70aHTSwMZa

Dutch ambassador points to photonics as potential field for cooperation

Japan has the parts of the stack that sound too boring (until the shipment is late) 4062.T Ibiden: the substrate floor under high-end AI packages. 2802.T Ajinomoto: the soup and MSG company behind Ajinomoto Build-up Film, the insulating film inside those substrates. 4063.T Shin-Etsu: wafers and materials before the chip is even a chip. 8035.T Tokyo Electron: process tools that make advanced manufacturing repeatable. 5332.T TOTO: ceramics. 3110.T Nittobo: glass fiber. 4004.T Resonac: specialty materials and packaging-adjacent chemistry. JSR matters too, but it is not a clean public ticker anymore after the 2024 delisting. That almost makes the point better imo. Some of the most important AI infrastructure is already disappearing from the easy screen. pattern is simple: AI looks American at the model layer. looks Taiwanese at the foundry layer. looks Korean at the memory layer. Then you go one layer lower and the map starts turning Japanese. Films. Fibers. Ceramics. Resists. Substrates. Tools. text:
Global X Healthtech ETF (HEAL) kinda sorta indirectly provides access to tattoo removal care. But it's mostly smart med equipment 

Or Roundhill Photonics & Optics ETF (LYTE) bc it's an ETF that holds companies that research and develop the technology used behind lasers themselves which would include laser removal tools.

It might...it might not 🤷‍♂️ I expect $LWLG to have another Negative day or two until the OBV hits it's Blue 2/1 Gann Fan Angle below.  If the price holds above $10, and it recovers above the Gann Fan Angles on both charts, it could go on another run.  Especially if the Photonics sector recovers as a whole.

user_name: Thoughts on Healthcare Markets and Tech
text:
The bottleneck framing is right, but the sequence being described here stops one layer short of where the pressure actually lands in high-stakes deployment contexts like healthcare.

Compute, then interconnect, then photonics, yes. But the binding constraint for clinical AI isn't just moving data between GPUs faster. It's the energy cost of inference at the point where the economics have to close against reimbursement rates. Nvidia's GB200 delivers roughly 30 times better performance per watt on certain inference tasks versus the H100 (their own published benchmarks). That number matters more in a clinical workflow than raw throughput, because you're not optimizing for speed, you're optimizing for cost-per-query against what Medicare will actually pay.

Photonics solves a real problem for hyperscale data centers routing between racks. It's a meaningful unlock for training clusters. But the constraint that actually determines whether a community hospital can run continuous real-time ICU monitoring (processing vitals, imaging, lab values, and unstructured notes simultaneously across a whole floor) is compute-per-watt at the edge, not optical interconnect bandwidth inside a data center someone else operates.

The pattern this post describes is accurate. Where it gets complicated is assuming the next constraint is singular. Interconnect matters for centralized inference at scale. Energy efficiency matters for distributed inference at the care delivery layer. Those aren't the same problem, and the companies solving one won't automatically solve the other.

The X-ray analogy is useful here: the constraint wasn't generating electricity, it was delivering reliable current to a specific room in a specific building (which took decades longer than generating it centrally).

https://t.co/l8PmUCFrKc

In reality, they are driven by bottlenecks.

Processors became faster, then memory became the", "id_str": "2060414438113788298", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "quote_count": 1, "reply_count": 61, "retweet_count": 54, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "2414734874"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [{"display_url": "cnbc.com/2026/05/29/nvi…", "expanded_url": "https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/29/nvidia-photonics-investment-ai.html", "indices": [1882, 1905], "url": "https://t.co/aNxbVdttHz"}], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjA0MTQ0Mzc4Nzg4OTQ1OTI=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "Most people think technology revolutions are driven by breakthroughs.

In reality, they are driven by bottlenecks.

The history of computing is a story of constraints moving from one layer of the technology stack to another. Processors became faster, then memory became the constraint. Networks improved, then bandwidth became the constraint. Data exploded, then storage and infrastructure became the constraints.

AI is following the same pattern.

For the last few years, the industry's biggest challenge was compute. The race was about building larger models, training them faster, and deploying more powerful chips. Nvidia solved that challenge better than anyone else, which is why it became one of the most valuable companies in the world.

When one constraint gets solved, another emerges.

That is what makes Nvidia's recent investments in Lumentum, Coherent, Marvell, Corning, and Ayar Labs so interesting. These investments suggest the company is already looking beyond compute and focusing on what could become the next major constraint in AI infrastructure.

As AI systems scale from thousands of GPUs to millions, the challenge shifts from generating intelligence to moving massive amounts of information quickly, efficiently, and economically across increasingly complex systems. The faster AI advances, the more important this challenge becomes.

This is why the photonics story matters. It is far more than a story about a new technology. It is a story about identifying a future constraint before it slows down an entire ecosystem.

The companies creating the most value over the next decade may be the ones building smarter AI models. They may also be the ones solving the hidden infrastructure challenges that make large-scale AI possible in the first place.

The best investors look for emerging technologies.

The best companies look for emerging constraints.

user_name: Thoughts on Healthcare Markets and Tech
text:
The bottleneck framing is right, but the sequence being described here stops one layer short of where the pressure actually lands in high-stakes deployment contexts like healthcare.

Compute, then interconnect, then photonics, yes. But the binding constraint for clinical AI isn't just moving data between GPUs faster. It's the energy cost of inference at the point where the economics have to close against reimbursement rates. Nvidia's GB200 delivers roughly 30 times better performance per watt on certain inference tasks versus the H100 (their own published benchmarks). That number matters more in a clinical workflow than raw throughput, because you're not optimizing for speed, you're optimizing for cost-per-query against what Medicare will actually pay.

Photonics solves a real problem for hyperscale data centers routing between racks. It's a meaningful unlock for training clusters. But the constraint that actually determines whether a community hospital can run continuous real-time ICU monitoring (processing vitals, imaging, lab values, and unstructured notes simultaneously across a whole floor) is compute-per-watt at the edge, not optical interconnect bandwidth inside a data center someone else operates.

The pattern this post describes is accurate. Where it gets complicated is assuming the next constraint is singular. Interconnect matters for centralized inference at scale. Energy efficiency matters for distributed inference at the care delivery layer. Those aren't the same problem, and the companies solving one won't automatically solve the other.

The X-ray analogy is useful here: the constraint wasn't generating electricity, it was delivering reliable current to a specific room in a specific building (which took decades longer than generating it centrally).

$AAOI $LITE

Space Chips + Optics

• $QRVO - RF chips
• $ADI - analog systems
• $AVGO - networking infrastructure
• $NVDA - AI + compute backbone
Critical Resources
• $MP - rare earths
• $FCX-copp

Mine for sure! 😉 He didn’t get into photonics until way later (I got $axti at $16). But he did better on some stocks (I sadly sold a lot of my $nbis even though I love that company, as I moved funds to some other stocks). And he has a lower DCA on a few.
But I have a lot more exposure to space 

I just started really focusing on investing last year in October. I have a lot more flexibility since I have my own business and he works pretty intense hours, so that helps me do a lot more research. But we’re not competitive with investing (just most other things!). We always celebrate each other’s wins

user_name: Sima Acan, MP for Oakville West
text:
In 1976 Taiwan sent 20 engineers abroad to learn chip-making from scratch. Decades later they control our global semiconductor supply chain. 

Today at committee, I asked: What can Canada learn from this?

We have our own unique Canadian Photonics Fabrication Centre, the talent, the resources, and the moment. The opportunity is now.

#ParliamentHill #HouseofCommons #OakvilleWest

text:
People still aren't bullish enough on the companies turning AI from prototype to deployed infrastructure.

The real moat isn't just the flashy model it's the lithography, photonics, and orbital comms that make scaling possible without melting the grid.

Names like $EUV (lithography/photonics), $DIPR (space/satcom), and $XA (cyber layer) sit at those chokepoints.

Early to Emerging stage. Market hasn't fully priced the physical world bottlenecks yet.

text:
Overall: it's good. Bumpy launch. Jury is still out if it will improve thru early access and be a solid buy.

Specifics: lacks creature comforts (invert y axis mouselook, key rebinding), has game breaking bugs (photonics goes black - all 3d rendering outside views go black).

text:
A major and largely overlooked fundamental catalyst for $SIVE is its strategic alliance with $ENAFF and O-Net Technologies.

This tripartite cooperation directly solves the biggest commercial bottleneck in silicon photonics: the highly complex and expensive optical packaging process, which historically accounted for up to 80% of total system costs.

Addressing the disinformation: I haven’t sold and don’t plan to sell a single share.

And I personally have plans to acquire more ownership + support https://t.co/Mb5eRuL2N7

text:
本人持有的 serenity系列的股票：

1. $sive： Wireless 做 5G 毫米波芯片（RFIC、波束成形），Photonics 做 III-V 族化合物半导体激光器件，用于光纤通信和 AI 数据中心光互连，位于cpo上游InP DFB 激光阵列的关键供应商。

2. $hmdpf ： 加拿大电力变压器制造商。

3. $sloif： 全球 SOI（绝缘体上硅）晶圆的领导者

4.  $lpk ： 核心技术是 LIDE（激光诱导深度蚀刻），可在玻璃上精密加工通孔而不开裂，被视为下一代 AI 芯片先进封装（玻璃基板）的关键制造设备提供商。

5. $alrib ：法国分子束外延（MBE）设备制造商，生产用于化合物半导体材料生长的 MBE 系统，是 III-V 族半导体（GaAs、InP、GaN 等）生产的上游设备供应商，客户涵盖科研机构和工业客户。

6. $iqe ：英国化合物半导体外延晶圆（epiwafer）的全球最大独立供应商

7. $towa ：日本半导体封装设备制造商。

8. foci ：台湾光纤无源器件及集成模块制造商，产品涵盖光纤连接器、耦合器、WDM、有源光缆（AOC）等

9. shunsin：台湾 SiP（系统级封装）模组制造商，是富士康旗下子公司，主营无线通信模组（RF 前端/WiFi）、光纤收发模组、MEMS 传感器等的封装测试，客户涵盖消费电子和云服务器领域。

小盘的就这些吧 都买的不多。

text:
CPO Supply Chain Participants (Bank of America):

$NVDA: 
- Two CPO-based systems: Quantum-X InfiniBand Photonics + Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics. 

$AVGO:
- Currently on 3rd generation of CPO-enabled switch ASIC w/ the TH6 Davisson released in H2'25

$MRVL: 
- Competes in CPO for both its XPU + switch ASIC businesses.
- Celestial acquisition gives stronger LT foothold in CPO; $AMZN is the first hyperscaler design-win for their PF chiplets.

$LITE: 
- In a great position to take advantage of the CPO inflection given its capabilities in optical components, In-house InP production.
- Multi-yr supply agreement w/ Nvidia in exchange for $2B equity is in the multibillion dollar sales range through 2030.

$COHR:
- Expects a $5B TAM for CPO.
- Received $2B equity from Nvidia in exchange for a multi-billion CPO supply agreement starting CY27 + going through CY30 (lasers, modules, etc.).

$BESI (BE Semiconductor):
- Leader in hybrid bonding + is exposed to CPO via its advanced die-attach and packaging solutions which are critical for integrating photonic chips w/ switch ASICs in CPO architectures.
- Besi noted that Nvidia + Broadcom switches use multiple hybrid bonding processes.

$AIXA (Aixtron):
- 90% share in epi equipment for optoelectronics (lasers + SiPh devices) for high-speed data communication.

$SOI (Soitec):
- Integrates photonic components on a silicon-on-insulator (SOI) + provides significant benefits for optical transceivers.
- E.g. faster data rates, cost efficiency, power savings, smaller size + better signal integrity.

Note: the above are direct summaries/quotes from BofA's report.

Total Optical TAM expansion of 5x:
2025 = $14B
2030 = $73B

With a CPO contribution of $15B.

That matches to Goldman's low-end CPO scenario - based on $NVDA Vera Rubin / https://t.co/7MtIiFxLcV

text:
@KobeissiLetter Its meaningless to cite this figure without understanding the fundamentals underpinning the awesome growth of photonics 👇

https://t.co/uF9nTiGKMX
embedded_text:
Insatiable demand, tightly constrained supply.     

The rush to upgrade optical networking between data centers, plus shortages of critical subcomponents, have left $CIEN with a $7b reported backlog in Q1 2026. 

Incredibly... 

that is ~146% of FY2025 revenue. https://t.co/ahQWyxgrMh

text: Great era in chip design. Feels like a post-Dennard-like phase but across the whole stack. Packaging, interconnects, photonics, substrates, power delivery, thermals all in the critical path. No single knob delivers performance anymore.

text:
$NOVT looks absolutely insane 😅

$5.5B Market Cap with an 11.73%-13.44% short float 
11.8 days to cover

Photonics & OEMs theme...
5 year compression on largest $ volumes ever, right over ATH's from over 20 years ago...

Looking like it wants to breakout here after last week's inside bar following the recent PEG, right over a major support that was accumulated on HVE back in July of 2022

Institutions have been building a small position here for quite some time and the current market environment (theme) + this daily & weekly setup is absolutely PRIMED. 
And a 10% short-float?! I mean, come on.

text:
let's reason from first principles on what this list is actually saying.

$AXTI up 761%. #SNDK up 523%. #AAOI up 421%. #AEHR up 371%. #NVTS up 310%.

this is not a meme list. this is a semiconductor materials and photonics sweep. every single name in the top 10 touches either compound semiconductors, optical interconnects, flash storage, or power electronics.

the market spent 2024 and 2025 arguing about which hyperscaler wins the ai race. meanwhile the actual winners were the people selling the shovels to build the picks to mine the gold.

#BE at 248%. #DOCN at 229%. #INTC at 225%. #MU at 163%.

even intel. INTEL. the company everyone wrote the obituary for. up 225%.

you'd have to actively avoid the data to have missed the infrastructure layer thesis. grok flagged compound semiconductors and optical interconnect as the underloved layer of the ai stack 18 months ago. the consensus was chasing software multiples.

the physics was always simple: you cannot move data at ai speeds without photonics. you cannot train at ai scale without memory bandwidth. #MU and #LITE were not mysteries. they were thermodynamics.

the analysts who were bearish on semis in early 2025 are very quiet right now. concerningly quiet.

text: I’ve been early on the photonics trade. I’m not questioning the trend or the technology, I question why a small Swedish company with literally ZERO proven track record in both order deliveries and manufacturing expansion and annual revenues in $30-40M and neg earnings should be valued at $3B. Competition in this field is fierce and will get even rougher. Size matters and a large player in this field should motivate a premium share price - NOT the other way around. That’s all.

text:
The AI infrastructure trade is beginning to rotate away from crowded GPU beta and into the hidden process control layers enabling advanced packaging and silicon photonics. 

$6830.TW is increasingly being reframed from a niche semiconductor analysis service company into a potential inspection and metrology proxy tied to CoWoS, InFO and optical interconnect scaling. 

As @rtk17025 points out, MSSCORPS may be one of Taiwan’s few domestic suppliers with exposure to high precision AOI and laser repair systems, giving it potential leverage to wafer defect inspection, photomasks, advanced packaging and silicon photonics. 

The key debate now is whether the company can successfully transition from a technical services model into a higher value “service + equipment + licensing” platform tied to Taiwan, Japan and U.S. silicon photonics IP.

text:
Solid take, Hunter...  $ALMU is flying under the radar. 15+ years in semis and interconnects here: GPUs steal the show, but moving massive data at scale is the real bottleneck — power, heat, and copper limits are killing efficiency. 

Aeluma’s III-V-on-silicon photonics on standard 200/300mm wafers could slash costs and scale like crazy vs. old InP tech. Strong partners (Tower, AIM, gov contracts) and real tech edge.

Early revenue, high execution risk, but at ~$414M cap, the upside in the AI optics wave is huge. 

Watch this one.Great thread. Adding to the list.(DYOR — small caps = high risk!)

text:
A team at Westlake University in China just published a handheld cancer detector in Nature Photonics. Single drop of blood, 94.9% accuracy for early-stage lung cancer, 10,000× more sensitive than the standard lab test.

Chip cost: $5.Worth paying attention to, but also worth reading carefully. That 94.9% comes from 171 patient samples. The device replaces refrigerator-sized lab equipment with an LED, a photodetector, and a 3D metamaterial chip. Genuinely impressive physics.

However, the gap nobody's talking about: every year produces 3-4 "revolutionary cancer detection" papers. Almost none survive the jump from Nature paper to clinical validation at scale.

Caveat here: 171 patients is a starting point, not proof. But the engineering approach is what's worth watching.

text: Massive gains often trigger regulatory scrutiny—expected given the timing of the Nasdaq dual-listing news. A "lottery ticket" position is one thing, but chasing this at 30x is a different game entirely.

text:
I just hit my first 10-bagger (+943%) on $SIVE in under 3 months! Discovered via @aleabitoreddit , a fundamental deep dive showed massive undervaluation in the photonics sector.

I initially bought 900 shares at 8.2 SEK, but the stock took off so fast I only managed to add a bit more below 20 SEK. The biggest lesson this investment taught me: trust your analysis and build a heavier position sooner when the conviction is there.

I locked in profits by trimming 1/3 at 49 SEK. With the price now around 85.55 SEK, I won't be adding at current valuations, but I am happily letting the rest ride as long as the company keeps delivering. Great investment, invaluable lessons.

text:
The market has figured out photonics. It’s catching on to power semis. But It still hasn’t woken up to fiber. 

The real chokepoint is sitting in the dirt. I'm positioned accordingly in $HLIT.

“Global fiber prices have risen ~70% from a 2021 trough of $3.70 to ~$6.30 per fiber-kilometer.”
 — Tom’s Hardware

“North American demand growth is projected at 22% to 25% this year, while supply expansion trails at 12% to 19%.”  
— Rebio Group estimates, reported by Tom’s Hardware

“That dual demand [AI + BEAD] elevates our fiber forecast, but it also makes the shortage more pronounced.”  
— Anis Khemakhem, Clearfield

“AI-focused data centers require significantly more fiber — about 36x more fiber than traditional CPU-based racks — to handle the massive data volumes and high-speed connectivity required by GPU clusters.”  
— Rahul Puri, CEO Optical Networking, STL

“Data center fiber demand grew roughly 76% year-on-year in 2025… the segment is projected to account for 30% of total global fiber demand by 2027. In 2024, that figure was below 5%.”
 — CRU data, reported by Tom’s Hardware

“AI has pushed fiber demand to levels we’ve never seen to ensure the incredible speeds inside data centers and between campuses. The many, massive data center builds we’re seeing today are driving huge volumes of intra-bay, inter-bay and middle-mile fiber.”
 — Anis Khemakhem, CCO, Clearfield

“The surge in hyperscale and AI network loads has significantly increased our expectations for fiber demand. Enterprise sales grew 58% year-over-year in Q3 2025, driven by continued strong adoption of Corning’s Gen AI products.”
 — John McGirr, Corning

“The U.S. will need to add 213.3 million more fiber miles by 2029, more than doubling its current amount from 159.6 million fiber miles to 372.9 million miles.”
 — Rahul Puri, STL

Major beneficiaries include:
$HLIT, $NOK, $LUMN, $CXDO, $CLFD, $BDC, $GLW

If AST SpaceMobile is the company building the connectivity layer from space, $HLIT (Harmonic) is its terrestrial mirror. The platform layer powers every fiber and cable network on the ground. Companies that build real technical moats inside secular spending waves, and get ignored while doing it, don’t stay ignored forever. AST taught me what that looks like before the re-rate. I’m seeing it again with Harmonic. And the timing matters. We’re at the front end of a global fiber supercycle, operators everywhere are being forced to densify, upgrade to DOCSIS 4.0, and push fiber deeper into their networks simultaneously. That spending wave is just starting to inflect, and Harmonic cOS is the platform sitting at the center of it. Companies that build real technical moats inside secular spending waves, and get ignored while doing it, don’t stay ignored forever. AST taught me what that looks like before the re-rate. I’m seeing it again with Harmonic. The operator accumulation flywheel is accelerating for Harmonic. Rest-of-market revenue surged 78% YoY in Q1, now 42% of total, up from near-zero a few years ago. New wins are landing every quarter across regional ISPs, international carriers, and fiber-first builders. This is the same pattern AST followed: anchor with tier-1 partners, prove the platform, then watch adoption compound as word travels through the operator community. Harmonic engineered a defined platform that creates a true moat legacy hardware vendors simply cannot replicate: • cOS™ — the industry’s first virtualized CMTS, replacing expensive proprietary hardware boxes with software running on generic servers • Unified DOCSIS + Fiber — a single stack managing both cable and fiber subscribers, the only platform that does this at scale • 150+ operators, 45.7M devices deployed — deep operational stickiness in billing, provisioning, and field workflows • 75% power reduction across seven successive hardware generations, iterative engineering lead nearly a decade in the making • AI-driven network intelligence, an entirely new growth vector built on top of the virtualized core This is full-stack vertical integration done right. The result? A company shedding its legacy video business ($145M divestiture closing Q2 2026) to emerge as a pure-play broadband software intelligence platform. Q1 2026 broadband revenue surged 43% YoY. Rest-of-market revenue, the diversification engine, grew 78%. Record $582M backlog, up 87% YoY. Management raised full-year guidance to $475–495M. EPS crushed estimates ($0.17 vs $0.10). In an environment where every ISP is racing to upgrade to DOCSIS 4.0 and deploy fiber, a platform that manages both from one stack is becoming table stakes, and $HLIT already has it. I’m positioned accordingly."}}}, "rest_id": "2058901649016627396", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "116010", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2058975546714046804", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "39448", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
A closer and objective look at the hard risks facing $POET Technologies:

While the hype around AI and silicon photonics has driven a massive rally, investors need to look closely at the underlying numbers and events before chasing the run.

First is the massive gap between the valuation and actual business results. The market cap has hovered around 2.4B USD, yet the company generated just over 500K USD in revenue last quarter. Meanwhile, they lost 12.3M USD, carrying an ongoing quarterly operational cash burn of nearly 9M USD.

Second is the severe history of share dilution. Shares outstanding exploded by over 300% in recent years. This was capped off by a massive 400M USD direct offering on May 15, which immediately dilutes existing holders and could expand the share base by up to 25% if the accompanying warrants are fully exercised.

Third is the operational instability and key customer losses. The stock crashed nearly 50% in late April after Marvell Semiconductor canceled its Celestial AI-related purchase orders. The reason given was a direct breach of confidentiality by POET's own management, who discussed order details during a public YouTube interview.

Fourth is the scathing short report alleging that the headline-grabbing 50M USD Lumilens order is with an empty corporate shell that has zero independent patents. Field reports from industry conferences also suggest key purported partners like Foxconn and Luxshare actually have inactive or non-existent relationships.

Finally, POET is facing multiple class-action lawsuits over these NDA disclosures and historical tax classification issues. Despite the optimistic commentary, even Wall Street analysts have an average target price of around 8.36, implying huge downside from current trading levels.

If you are playing this strictly as a short-term momentum trade, chasing the run is a perfectly fine strategy as long as you keep a tight stop loss and do not get married to the position. But for anyone looking at this as a long-term investment, the operational track record, structural dilution, and relationship failures make it an incredibly high-risk bet at these valuations.

Today’s photonics/AI infra tape is splitting hard: $DELL is the standout, up ~33%, and $AVGO is up roughly 5% as investors crowd into scaled AI server/networking winners. 

Meanwhile, the higher-beta optics names are cooling off, with $AAOI down about 6% and $AXTI down around 11% after big moves. 

$SIVE is still volatile but positive on the Stockholm tape, recently showing around +4% on the day - but down in the U.S. markets.

Let’s see how things play out! 

I’m still bullish on all. 🐂

text:
Nvidia just bet $6.5 BILLION on light-based data transfer to fix AI's biggest bottleneck ⚡

Photonics could replace copper wiring in data centers — faster, cooler, more efficient.

The AI infrastructure race is getting interesting. #AI #Nvidia #Tech #Innovation #DataCenters https://t.co/CAyldoD2Kp

Interesting upstream read through. CPO doesn’t integrate lasers into the package. Both Broadcom and NVIDIA use external pluggable laser modules (ELSFP standard) that feed light into the silicon photonics engines. Bailly uses 16 per switch; Quantum-X manages all 144 × 800G channels with just 18. ￼

10,000 Foxconn switch trays = ~160,000 ELSFP modules this year at Bailly-equivalent counts.

The ELSFP supply chain is a two-horse race. $LITE announced its largest PO ever for ELSFPs and $COHR announced one too. ￼ Both targeting volume into scale-up hardware by 2027.

AI Photonics

- $MRVL "CPO units [in the Market] from 1MM in 2025 to more than 100 MM by 2030"

- Foxconn: "We will ship around 10k CPO units this year and the shipment next year will grow several times" 

$LITE $AAOI $GLW

text:
Nvidia has committed at least $6.5 billion since March 2026 to photonics companies as it pushes to replace copper-based data links with light-based infrastructure for AI systems that are straining existing capacity. The investment spree spans $2 billion stakes in Coherent and Lumentum tied to multiyear supply and capacity agreements, a $2 billion investment in Marvell Technology focused on custom chips and silicon photonics, participation in Ayar Labs’ $500 million Series E, and a $500 million investment in Corning to expand U.S. fiber manufacturing capacity. At GTC, CEO Jensen Huang said co-packaged optics are central to Nvidia’s Spectrum-X Ethernet platform, as the company bets that silicon photonics will be key to scaling AI compute while managing power constraints.

FOUNDER: Jensen Huang

AMOUNT: $6,500,000,000

#VentureCapital #NVidia #JensenHuang #TradedVC

text:
This is the old debate: should I invest in an enabler or beneficiary?

Meaning: 

Does this company aid the AI buildout, or just use AI to improve its service? 

Examples of enablers are semis, memory, neoclouds, photonics. The picks and shovels. Beneficiaries are fintech, SaaS, healthcare, etc..

Right now, enablers are gaining all of the value.

$SIVE is up 1000% in 2 moths.
$MU is up 200% in 2 months.

Yet the beneficiaries are losing their value.

$ZETA is down 50% in 2 years.
$DUOL is down 80% in 1 year.

What's happening to the beneficiaries? They're going down because investors think AI will replace their business.

Every investor is screaming "SaaS is dead."

But that's really only for the vibe coded SaaS companies, not the incumbents.

It seems to be the case that switching costs are too high and this gives them time to add all the features that your favorite startup flexes on Twitter.

That is why so many of these companies in the beneficiary niche are undervalued.

Also, let's not forget, with AI, it actually allows these companies to produce more with less cost -> actually benefiting them overall.

Not a bad place to be buying these names for the long-term imo:

$WDAY (Workday inc)
$NOW (ServiceNow inc)
$SAP (Sap IE)
$CRM (Salesforce inc)
$TEAM (Atlassian Corp)
$DUOL (Duolingo inc) 
$ZETA (Zeta Global)
$INTU (Intuit inc)
$VEEV (Veeva Systems inc)

And more.

Also, I'm not saying enablers will go down.

I'm invested in a lot of them.

I'm just saying the hate on beneficiaries is not deserved.

Yes, some will die, but most of them with moats, will continue to push forward.

And actually, produce more efficiently in the future, all while it is at a discounted price today.

This is just my 2c.

- Con

The LIDE patent portfolio is dense and covers the core process (Laser-Induced Deep Etching) for forming Through-Glass Vias (TGVs) without micro-cracking. This matters because glass is so brittle that conventional drilling (mechanical or standard laser ablation) creates tiny fault lines. When the chip heats up, those fault lines expand and shatter the substrate. LIDE solves this at the physics level. Competitor check: Canon has TGV tools but uses a different process. JNTC (Korea) has proprietary in-house tech but explicitly avoids LPKF-style equipment. Corning has laser capabilities but is a glass maker, not a tool vendor. No other company has a production-proven LIDE equivalent. Customer lock-in: CEO Klaus Fiedler stated that more than 80% of major global semiconductor customers have selected LPKF equipment for test/R&D environments. Re-qualification of a different TGV formation method would take 1-2 years and require re-running all reliability testing. Toll booth confirmation: An Intel patent (granted 2025) explicitly describes LIDE-formed TGVs being restructured for optical waveguides in a CPO architecture. This is the definition of being designed into the process flow. Widening moat: LPKF is expanding beyond TGV formation into glass-package singulation, multilayer glass-stack laser bonding, and CPO-on-glass integration. They are moving from one tool in the process to potentially three or four. We are so early. text:
Global semiconductor equipment sales, the lithography, metrology, and photonics tools that build every chip, are projected to hit a record $133 billion in 2025 and $156 billion by 2027. 

Every AI breakthrough traces back to a fab, and every fab traces back to the equipment that builds it, which is exactly where this spending lands. 

Source: SEMI projections, December 2025

text:
翻译

$SIVE(Sivers Semiconductors)是我心目中最具吸引力的 CPO / 光子学(photonics)敞口标的。

回应一下虚假信息:我一股都没卖,也不打算卖任何一股。

我确实认为这家公司有一天会从现在约 21 亿美元市值,成长为下一个 800 亿美元以上的 $LITE(Lumentum)。

而且我个人有计划继续增持,并支持他们的并购(M&A)前景。

我相信财报电话会议纪要(earnings transcripts)将非常正面。

因为在过去几个月里,我们已经陆续发现以下信息:

AlChip(世芯)/ Amazon(亚马逊)的定向增发,对 Ayar Labs 是利好 → 对 $SIVE 而言意味着被设计进 Trainium 4(亚马逊第四代自研 AI 芯片)。

纬颖(Wiwynn)+ Ayar 的 CPO 放量。

$JBL(捷普 / Jabil)1.6T 光收发器爬坡,Sivers 的进展比市场预期更快(具备相对显著的护城河 + 有多少产能客户就要多少需求)。

O-Net(昂纳科技)与 $SIVE 一起扩大 ELS(外部光源)工作规模。
$YSS(York Space Systems)收购了 $SIVE 的 Allspace 主要合作伙伴,正把 Sivers 设计进太空国防主承包商(Space defense primes)。

$SIVE 获得新的 CHIPS 法案(CHIPS ACT)资助。

$POET(POET Technologies)下半年放量,以及他们新拿到的从 5,000 万美元上调到 5 亿美元的订单(以 $SIVE 作为光源)。

信息发现:$AAPL(苹果)正在为下一代消费电子设备使用 $SIVE 激光器。
信息发现:与 Lightelligence(已上市,市值 100 亿美元以上)+ Lightmatter 的关联,二者可能是客户。

Celestial(Celestial AI)放量,$MRVL(美满电子 / Marvell)给出指引信号。

新客户正在与 $SIVE 合作 TFLN(薄膜铌酸锂)方案,例如 Lightium。

$AMD(超微)选择 $GFS(格芯)做 CPO,而 GFS 将 Sivers 列为两家激光供应商之一。

Ayar 从官网上撤下了 $MTSI(MACOM)/ $LITE(Lumentum),并把 $SIVE 标示为主要供应商 / 独家供应源。

Ayar 为放量爬坡融资 5 亿美元(英特尔、联发科、英伟达、AMD 等参与)。
2025 年年报释出可插拔(pluggable)TAM(总潜在市场)扩张的信号。

预计很快在纳斯达克(Nasdaq)上市。

被纳入 MSCI 小盘指数 / Nasdaq OMX 指数,使贝莱德(BlackRock)、先锋(Vanguard)等成为被动买入者。
2025 年年报释出并购信号 + 两位拥有该领域经验的新董事会成员加入。

$NOK(诺基亚)在 2025 年年报中显示为可能的客户。
$LITE 因 EML 合同导致 CW 产能瓶颈,而 $SIVE 释出已与稳懋(WIN Semiconductor)达成产能协议的信号,使其有望成为 CPO 板块的瓶颈所有者 + 咽喉控制点(chokepoint)。

以上所有市场研究都是在财报发布之前完成的。

任何财报结果都只是对已完成的供应链映射的确认而已。

我认为没人会在乎上一季度的营收数字,因为 $SIVE 是一只极具吸引力的、押注 2027 年(尤其是下半年起)的多头标的。

我现在唯一关注的事情有:

整体光子学超级周期(photonics supercycle)的 TAM 扩张(例如光学引擎、ELS、可插拔模块),无论是来自并购还是技术发展。

现有公司的放量爬坡预期。

纳斯达克上市时间表,以便为公司的并购努力提供更多流动性支撑。
任何关于 CPO / 可插拔领域释出的新客户信号。
embedded_text:
$SIVE is the most compelling CPO/photonics exposure to me.

Addressing the disinformation: I haven’t sold and don’t plan to sell a single share. 

I do think this ends up the next $80B+ $LITE one day from ~$2.1B.

And I personally have plans to acquire more ownership + support https://t.co/Mb5eRuL2N7

[1461, 1466], "text": "SIVE"}], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjAwNzE4NzYxODYyNTk0NTc=", "text": "翻译 $SIVE(Sivers Semiconductors)是我心目中最具吸引力的 CPO / 光子学(photonics)敞口标的。 回应一下虚假信息:我一股都没卖,也不打算卖任何一股。 我确实认为这家公司有一天会从现在约 21 亿美元市值,成长为下一个 800 亿美元以上的 $LITE(Lumentum)。 而且我个人有计划继续增持,并支持他们的并购(M&A)前景。 我相信财报电话会议纪要(earnings transcripts)将非常正面。 因为在过去几个月里,我们已经陆续发现以下信息: AlChip(世芯)/ Amazon(亚马逊)的定向增发,对 Ayar Labs 是利好 → 对 $SIVE 而言意味着被设计进 Trainium 4(亚马逊第四代自研 AI 芯片)。 纬颖(Wiwynn)+ Ayar 的 CPO 放量。 $JBL(捷普 / Jabil)1.6T 光收发器爬坡,Sivers 的进展比市场预期更快(具备相对显著的护城河 + 有多少产能客户就要多少需求)。 O-Net(昂纳科技)与 $SIVE 一起扩大 ELS(外部光源)工作规模。 $YSS(York Space Systems)收购了 $SIVE 的 Allspace 主要合作伙伴,正把 Sivers 设计进太空国防主承包商(Space defense primes)。 $SIVE 获得新的 CHIPS 法案(CHIPS ACT)资助。 $POET(POET Technologies)下半年放量,以及他们新拿到的从 5,000 万美元上调到 5 亿美元的订单(以 $SIVE 作为光源)。 信息发现:$AAPL(苹果)正在为下一代消费电子设备使用 $SIVE 激光器。 信息发现:与 Lightelligence(已上市,市值 100 亿美元以上)+ Lightmatter 的关联,二者可能是客户。 Celestial(Celestial AI)放量,$MRVL(美满电子 / Marvell)给出指引信号。 新客户正在与 $SIVE 合作 TFLN(薄膜铌酸锂)方案,例如 Lightium。 $AMD(超微)选择 $GFS(格芯)做 CPO,而 GFS 将 Sivers 列为两家激光供应商之一。 Ayar 从官网上撤下了 $MTSI(MACOM)/ $LITE(Lumentum),并把 $SIVE 标示为主要供应商 / 独家供应源。 Ayar 为放量爬坡融资 5 亿美元(英特尔、联发科、英伟达、AMD 等参与)。 2025 年年报释出可插拔(pluggable)TAM(总潜在市场)扩张的信号。 预计很快在纳斯达克(Nasdaq)上市。 被纳入 MSCI 小盘指数 / Nasdaq OMX 指数,使贝莱德(BlackRock)、先锋(Vanguard)等成为被动买入者。 2025 年年报释出并购信号 + 两位拥有该领域经验的新董事会成员加入。 $NOK(诺基亚)在 2025 年年报中显示为可能的客户。 $LITE 因 EML 合同导致 CW 产能瓶颈,而 $SIVE 释出已与稳懋(WIN Semiconductor)达成产能协议的信号,使其有望成为 CPO 板块的瓶颈所有者 + 咽喉控制点(chokepoint)。 以上所有市场研究都是在财报发布之前完成的。 任何财报结果都只是对已完成的供应链映射的确认而已。 我认为没人会在乎上一季度的营收数字,因为 $SIVE 是一只极具吸引力的、押注 2027 年(尤其是下半年起)的多头标的。 我现在唯一关注的事情有: 整体光子学超级周期(photonics supercycle)的 TAM 扩张(例如光学引擎、ELS、可插拔模块),无论是来自并购还是技术发展。 现有公司的放量爬坡预期。 纳斯达克上市时间表,以便为公司的并购努力提供更多流动性支撑。 任何关于 CPO / 可插拔领域释出的新客户信号。"}}}, text:
Been doing a lot of reading on the supply chain this year. I am not always right. But the ones that were right, were very right.

$IQE: 17p → 48 (only independent epiwafer foundry globally, photonics supply chain bottleneck)

$SOI: €70 → €192 (silicon photonics substrate near monopoly for AI data centers)

$SIVE: SEK 30 → SEK 70 (CPO laser source, InP chokepoint in the AI optical stack)

$ENSI: 72p → 116p (ASIC design house, AI data centers + Space + Post-Quantum)

$LBIRD: €19→ €26.5 (European defense laser leader, NATO rearmament cycle)

$CIFR: $15 → $25 (Bitcoin miner transitioning to AI data center operator, $9.3B contracted)

$ONDS: $8 → $13.6 (autonomous drone prime contractor, +1,065% Q1 YoY revenue)

$LPK: €18 → €24 (only proven glass substrate LIDE process globally, AI packaging)

None of these were obvious at entry. All had real risks. Most were ignored.

The market repricing confirms the reading.

text:
Looks like we agree on the similarities between $CPSH and $SIVE when it was just starting to break out...

Completely different businesses, but both are phenomenal chokepoint plays.

Back when $SIVE was sitting at ~8 SEK, most people saw it as just another small-cap photonics name.

Little did they know, it was hugely undervalued as a supplier sitting at a genuine bottleneck in the optical interconnect space... exactly the layer AI needed as data movement became the constraint.

The market eventually caught on and it proceeded to ~10x.

$CPSH feels like it's at that same pre-recognition stage, sitting at the same valuation as $SIVE before it took off.

> $SIVE: photonics/optical interconnects for AI datacenters

> $CPSH: AlSiC thermal management for defense, space, and potentially AI chips

Both are critical suppliers that get overlooked until market demand forces the re-rating.

And remember, $CPSH is the only meaningful US-based vertically integrated AlSiC supplier, with sole-source status on many applications per their own 10-K...

When the market starts to recognize this as a true bottleneck, it'll re-rate hard and fast.

text:
PHOTONICS 101: How AI data moves at the speed of light.

Every prompt sent to ChatGPT travels through five layers of photonic infrastructure before a single token returns.

Why photons beat electrons:
Copper electrons hit a thermal wall above 800G. Photons don't.
Light doesn't heat the medium. 80% less power. The only path forward at 1.6T+.

The stack, from raw wafer to AI datacenter:

01 · Foundation — Wafers and photonic foundries. Where light meets silicon.
$TSM, $GFS, $ASML

02 · Components — Lasers, modulators, detectors. The active parts that generate, encode, and read light.
$MRVL, $POET, $SIVE

03 · Modules — Optical transceivers and light engines. Convert electrical to optical. The CPO transition lives here.
$LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, $JBL

04 · Systems — Switches and networking gear. Route optical traffic between racks. Where 800G is becoming 1.6T right now.
$ANET, $CIEN, $CSCO

05 · Deployment — AI datacenters and hyperscalers. $725B in 2026 capex. Every GPU rack runs on optical interconnects.
$NVDA, $MSFT, $GOOGL, $META

Every layer wins together. Hyperscalers buy more switches. Switches need more transceivers. Transceivers need more lasers. Lasers need more wafers.

The AI capex flywheel turns the entire stack.

Five layers. One supercycle.

text:
Germans are so technical and precise that certain mistakes are only considered stupidity.

In an MSc lab course, I heard about an international student who was trying to build a laser in the lab as part of a photonics coursework. This student was not wearing safety goggles but (s)he was supposed to. (S)he looked directly into the laser beam and got injured — got blind. To be honest, I only heard the story from my photonics professor, so I have no idea whether the student was compensated by insurance in Germany.

During a laser safety briefing, my photonics professor shared the story and said that it was not an accident. He used a German term which technically means, “you stupidly injured yourself.”

I asked why, and the professor responded: “If you know something is going to hurt you and you go ahead and do it anyway, then get hurt, that’s not an accident — that’s stupidity. And there’s a German word for that.”

Germany doesn’t have time for your stupidity! Beware⚠️

text:
我在5月11日最高点清空 $MU 
$ARM 在5月20日我加仓的callouts之后up 27% 
$SIVE 在我5月21日的callouts之后up 50% 

我打算在 $SIVE 5月29日 earning之前清掉我大部分的仓位
$SIVE是小盘photonics，历史波动率极高 
财报是binary event 
故事已经被炒得太满 
更关键的是它还在ramp阶段
激光阵列、模块产能、订单兑现，全是execution risk 
任何一个timeline推迟，或者财报里提一句要融资 
市场立刻会有反应

我会share everything here
Stay Tuned
